metadata.purpose: Cardiac disease epidemiology relevant to CVB (Coxsackievirus B) causation
primary_sources: Caforio ALP et al., Eur Heart J 2013;34:2636-2648 (myocarditis clinical guidance)
Kindermann I et al., JACC 2012;59:779-792 (myocarditis outcomes)
Grist NR & Bell EJ, Prog Med Virol 1974;18:114-157 (CVB heart disease epidemiology)
Woodruff JF, Am J Pathol 1980;101:427-484 (viral myocarditis review)
Fung G et al., Circ Res 2016;118:496-514 (CVB3 pathogenesis)
Basso C et al., Lancet 2009;374:305-317 (cardiomyopathy etiology)
Richardson P et al., Lancet 1996;348:1070-1075 (DCM definition/incidence)
Dec GW & Fuster V, NEJM 1994;331:1564-1575 (idiopathic DCM)
Limas CJ et al., Circulation 1990;82:2220-2226 (viral DCM)
Pauschinger M et al., Circulation 1999;99:889-895 (viral genomes in DCM)
Bowles NE et al., Circulation 2003;107:2335-2340 (viral genomes in pediatric cardiomyopathy)
Mason JW et al., NEJM 1995;333:269-275 (immunosuppression trial in myocarditis)
McCarthy RE et al., NEJM 2000;342:690-695 (long-term outcome of myocarditis)
Felker GM et al., NEJM 2000;342:1077-1084 (myocarditis outcomes in transplant)
Frustaci A et al., J Am Coll Cardiol 2003;41:1072-1076 (immunohistology in DCM)
Kuhl U et al., Circulation 2005;111:887-893 (viral persistence and ventricular dysfunction)
Kuhl U et al., J Am Coll Cardiol 2005;45:270-280 (DCM and viral myocarditis overlap)

myocarditis.definition: Inflammation of the myocardium confirmed by histology (Dallas criteria) or cardiac MRI (Lake Louise criteria)

general_population_per_100k_per_year.note: Clinical presentation-based; true incidence higher due to subclinical cases
general_population_per_100k_per_year.source: Cooper 2009 NEJM; Caforio 2013 Eur Heart J

autopsy_prevalence_percent.note: Undiagnosed myocarditis at autopsy in sudden cardiac death cohorts

suspected_vs_confirmed_ratio.note: ~10 clinically suspected cases per 1 histologically confirmed; most are diagnosed by CMR

fraction_attributable_to_viruses_percent.note: Varies by era and geography; higher with PCR-based detection

coxsackievirus_fraction_of_viral_myocard.note: CVB historically dominant; more recent series show parvovirus B19 and HHV-6 overtaking CVB in developed countries

coxsackievirus_fraction_of_all_myocardit.note: Derived: 50% viral × 25-50% CVB within viral = ~12-25%; central estimate 22%

cvb_serotypes.CVB3: Most common cardiac serotype; direct cytolytic and immune-mediated damage
cvb_serotypes.CVB4: Second most common; also infects beta-cells (T1DM link)

enteroviruses_combined_percent.note: Includes CVB + other enteroviruses (echovirus, EV-A71 etc.)

peak_age_years.note: Most common in young adults; but affects all ages

age_distribution.neonatal_fulminant_risk: Highest case-fatality ratio (>50% untreated) in neonatal CVB myocarditis

male_to_female_ratio.note: Males overrepresented ~2:1; estrogen may be cardioprotective; testosterone exacerbates CVB3 cardiac damage in mouse models
male_to_female_ratio.source: Caforio 2013; Woodruff 1980; GSE35182 (sex differences in CVB3 myocarditis mouse study)

progression_to_dcm_percent.note: Subset with persistent LV dysfunction; higher with viral persistence on biopsy

death_or_transplant_5_year_percent.note: Range reflects heterogeneous cohorts; fulminant myocarditis has paradoxically better long-term prognosis

short_term_mortality_percent.note: Without mechanical circulatory support; dramatically improved with IABP/ECMO

in_hospital_mortality_overall_percent.note: General myocarditis admission; higher for cardiogenic shock

myocarditis_in_young_scd_percent.note: Found at autopsy in sudden cardiac death in athletes and young adults

seasonal_patterns.mechanism: Enteroviral transmission peaks coincide with late-summer/autumn enterovirus seasons
seasonal_patterns.note: More pronounced in northern hemisphere temperate climates; less seasonal in tropics

geographic_variation.asia_pacific: Higher apparent incidence in reported series; may reflect ascertainment bias or true higher CVB circulation
geographic_variation.developing_countries: Likely underdiagnosed; perinatal CVB myocarditis major cause of infant mortality

dilated_cardiomyopathy.definition: Left ventricular dilatation and systolic dysfunction in absence of coronary artery disease or abnormal loading conditions

idiopathic_percent.note: Many 'idiopathic' cases likely post-viral when investigated thoroughly

viral_post_inflammatory_percent.note: Confirmed post-viral DCM by biopsy/serology; true fraction higher

viral_genome_in_dcm_biopsy_percent.note: PCR detection of viral genomes in endomyocardial biopsy in DCM patients

prior_myocarditis_in_dcm_percent.note: Histological or serological evidence of prior myocarditis in DCM patients

viral_persistence_worsens_lv_function.finding: In Kuhl 2005, patients with viral genome persistence at 6 months had worse LVEF compared to clearance group (mean LVEF -3.8% vs +5.5%)

antiviral_clearance_improves_lv_function.finding: Interferon-beta treatment that achieved viral clearance associated with LVEF improvement of +10-14% units at 6 months
antiviral_clearance_improves_lv_function.source: Kuhl 2005 J Am Coll Cardiol; Kindermann 2012

5_year_mortality_viral_etiology_vs_other.note: Viral etiology slightly worse prognosis due to inflammatory component

cvb_attributable_fraction_dcm.calculation: P(CVB positive) × RR reduction if CVB cleared = 0.20 × 0.60 ≈ 0.12
cvb_attributable_fraction_dcm.note: Assumes 20% DCM has CVB genomes; viral clearance reduces DCM progression by ~60%

estimated_preventable_us_dcm_cases_per_y.calculation: 7 per 100k incidence × 320M US population × 0.12 = ~2,688 cases prevented / 100k × 320M × 0.12

pericarditis.definition: Inflammation of the pericardium; 'idiopathic' (presumed viral) in ~85% of cases in developed countries

general_population_per_100k_per_year.note: 5% of ED chest pain presentations; most common pericardial disease

assumed_viral_or_idiopathic_percent.note: In developed-world series; tuberculosis dominant in developing countries

cvb_specifically_percent.note: CVB implicated serologically; lower than myocarditis since many agents cause pericarditis

constrictive_pericarditis_development_pe.note: Rare complication; higher risk with bacterial etiology

us_estimates_per_year.note: Conservative estimate; likely undercounts subclinical CVB cardiac involvement

assumptions: 70% vaccine efficacy against all CVB serotypes
Effective vaccination reduces CVB circulation by 60% (herd immunity partial)
Conservative: does not account for DCM prevented by preventing myocarditis progression

acute_phase_days_1_to_14.cell_death_mechanism: Lytic infection of cardiomyocytes; VP1 protease cleaves dystrophin
acute_phase_days_1_to_14.immune_activation: TLR3/MDA5 → IFN-beta → NK and CD8+ T cell infiltration

chronic_phase_days_14_plus.lv_dysfunction: Progressive dilatation; wall thinning; reduced EF
chronic_phase_days_14_plus.source: Kuhl 2005; Caforio 2013; GDS4436/GDS4311 (CVB3 chronic myocarditis microarray)

human_dcm.notable_finding: GSE19303 (81 human samples) shows overlapping inflammatory signature between post-viral and familial DCM

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: CxVB3 infection and cardiac function
primary_cvb_cardiac.design: CVB3 vs PBS control; time points 3, 9, 30 days
primary_cvb_cardiac.relevance: Classic time-course dataset for CVB3 cardiac gene expression

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: Sex differences during acute myocarditis against CVB3
primary_cvb_cardiac.design: Male vs female; CVB3 infected vs mock; 2 time points

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: CVB3 infection of AJ, B10.A, CSS3 and B6.chr3AJ (heart tissue)
primary_cvb_cardiac.relevance: Genetic susceptibility loci for CVB3 myocarditis

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: Sex differences during innate immune response against CVB3
primary_cvb_cardiac.relevance: Innate immune sex differences in CVB3 heart infection

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: CVB3 infection of 129S1 and 129X1 (heart tissue day 6)
Soluble ST2 induces cardiac dysfunction in fulminant myocarditis
primary_cvb_cardiac.relevance: Recent RNA-seq; ST2/IL-33 pathway in CVB fulminant myocarditis

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: GPR15-mediated T cell recruitment during acute viral myocarditis
primary_cvb_cardiac.relevance: T cell trafficking; GPR15 checkpoint; acute CVB3 myocarditis

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: Dissecting Cellular Landscape in Viral Myocarditis by scRNA-seq
primary_cvb_cardiac.relevance: Single-cell resolution of cell types in CVB3 myocarditis

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: hiPSC-derived cardiomyocytes as in vitro model for CVB3 myocarditis
primary_cvb_cardiac.relevance: HUMAN cardiomyocyte response to CVB3; drug screening platform

primary_cvb_cardiac.title: Enhanced RNAi does not provide efficient antiviral immunity (spleen CVB3)

dcm_human_expression.title: Myocardial gene expression in patients with dilated cardiomyopathy
dcm_human_expression.relevance: Large human DCM dataset; includes post-viral and familial DCM

dcm_human_expression.title: Gene expression profile of human heart failure from different etiologies
dcm_human_expression.relevance: Multi-etiology heart failure including viral DCM

dcm_human_expression.title: Myocardial gene expression profiles and autoantibodies predict DCM progression
dcm_human_expression.relevance: Autoantibody-mediated DCM; relevant to post-CVB autoimmunity

not_downloaded_reason.reason: 404 Not Found — likely superseries; sub-series need individual download
GDS accession (not GSE); data accessible via linked GSE series

cvb3_heart_rnaseq_eur.note: European (EBI/ENA) accessions; linked to GSE248521 or related study

viral_myocarditis_mouse.design: Healthy controls (n=2) vs myocarditis (n=2); Mus musculus; RNA-Seq
viral_myocarditis_mouse.note: Bulk RNA-seq comparison; linked to GSE174458
